origin.name: Sierra Leone
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Births, Deaths and Marriages Registry (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Freetown guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Sierra Leone is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Births, Deaths and Marriages Registry (English language)
special_rules: Sierra Leone issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance from the Sierra Leone Police is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Sierra Leonean authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Sierra Leonean documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Freetown can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Sierra Leone to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Sierra Leone. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Births registry, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
